subject: Databases
title: Secondary Indexes in Partitions
description: A quick, practical guide to Secondary Indexes in Partitions.

hook.text: Data is partitioned by user_id. You need to query by email. The email index must span all partitions.

buildup.text: Local indexes live on each partition. A query by email hits every partition to find the right one. Scatter-gather.

discovery.text: Global indexes partition the index separately. Email queries hit one index partition, then one data partition.

twist.text: Global indexes must be updated on every write. Cross-partition index updates add latency to writes.

climax.text: Choose local for write-heavy workloads, global for read-heavy. There's no free lunch.

punchline.text: Local index: fast writes, slow reads. Global index: slow writes, fast reads.

quiz.question: What is the trade-off between local and global secondary indexes?
options: Local indexes have fast writes but require scatter-gather reads; global indexes have fast reads but slower writes
Local indexes take more disk space
Global indexes don't support unique constraints
